shell.clamped: Showing the most recent 366 days of the selected range.
shell.subject: insights

savedViews.title: Saved views
savedViews.empty: Save the period and filters you keep coming back to — “Weekly retro”, “My deep work”… Views are yours alone and follow you across devices.
savedViews.unreadable: This view was saved by a newer version and can't be applied here.

filters.title: Filters
filters.inertNote: The You tab shows only your own nights, so these filters don't apply there. They still shape the other tabs.
filters.includeInactiveHint: Count grayed-out time such as sleep

customize.title: Customize overview
customize.description: Pick which cards show and in what order. This is your layout — your partner keeps theirs.
customize.lockedNote: Total, daily average, and active days always lead the overview.

empty.title: No tracked time in this period
empty.description: Insights count timed events from your calendar. Schedule something in this range — or pick a different period — to see where your time goes.

delta.newSr: new — nothing in the previous period

lede.overviewHeadline: You tracked {total}{direction, select, up {, up {pct}% ({magnitude}) vs the previous {unit, select, week {week} month {month} other {period}}} down {, down {pct}% ({magnitude}) vs the previous {unit, select, week {week} month {month} other {period}}} level {, level with the previous {unit, select, week {week} month {month} other {period}}} other {}}.
lede.trendsNoneHeadline: Not enough history yet to call a trend.
lede.trendsNoneSupport: Keep tracking and a direction will show up here.
lede.trendsHeadline: {direction, select, flat {Your tracked time is holding steady across the period.} other {Your tracked time is trending {direction}{hasRate, select, yes {, about {sign}{rate} per {granularity, select, day {day} week {week} other {month}}} other {}}.}}
lede.patternsSupportDaypart: Your best-rated stretch of the day: {daypart}.
lede.tasksOverdueHeadline: {count, plural, one {# task is} other {# tasks are}} overdue and still open.
lede.tasksDoneHeadline: You finished {count, plural, one {# task} other {# tasks}} in this period{direction, select, more {, {diff} more than the previous {unit, select, week {week} month {month} other {period}}} fewer {, {diff} fewer than the previous {unit, select, week {week} month {month} other {period}}} other {}}.
lede.tasksDoneSupport: You finished {count, plural, one {# task} other {# tasks}} in this period.
lede.tasksAdherenceSupport: {pct}% of due tasks landed on time.

overview.perDayHeadline: {trend, select, none {{total} tracked this period.} level {{total} tracked, level with the previous period.} up {{total} tracked, up {pct}% vs the previous period.} other {{total} tracked, down {pct}% vs the previous period.}}
overview.perDayFootnoteTypical: Dashed line: typical day ({typical}) · curve: trailing 7-day average · tap a day for detail
overview.perDayFootnote: Curve: trailing 7-day average · tap a day for detail
overview.shiftPoints: {name}: share {direction, select, up {up} other {down}} {points} points vs previous period

trends.emptyDescription: Timed events in this period will appear here as a trend across days, weeks, or months.
trends.footnoteAvg: Curve: trailing 7-day average · tap a day for detail
trends.tableCaption: Total tracked time per context for {label}
trends.byContextAria: Tracked time per context per {granularity}, top {count} contexts

patterns.emptyDescription: Weekday and hour-of-day patterns need at least one timed event in this period.
patterns.srPeak: Average tracked time peaks on {day} at {ms} per day.
patterns.srBlocks: The period splits into {count} busy blocks, typically {ms} long.
patterns.tooltipTotal: {total} total over {count, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}
patterns.attributesEmpty: Set focus, energy or satisfaction on events to unlock attribute patterns — deep-work share, your best time of day, and how demanding your days run.
patterns.deepWorkEmptyHint: rate focus on events to track this
patterns.energyEmptyHint: rate energy on events to track this

tasks.emptyDescription: Create tasks in a collection and their throughput shows up here.
tasks.srSummary: {completed} tasks completed and {created} created in {label}. {overdue} open tasks are overdue.
tasks.topLevelNote: Top-level tasks only; subtasks count toward their parent.

balance.contextMixAria: Stacked tracked time per context per {granularity}, {label}
balance.shareShiftsCaption: Context shares of tracked time, this period vs the previous one
balance.satisfactionEmpty: Rate satisfaction on at least {min} events in a context to see which parts of your time feel best, looking back.
balance.satisfactionAria: {name}: mean satisfaction {mean} of 4, from {n} rated items

optimize.mutedDescription: You won't see this kind of tip again, on any device.
optimize.srSuggestions: {count, plural, one {# suggestion} other {# suggestions}} for this period.
optimize.coverageBody: {withAttributes} of {tracked} timed events this period carry a detail — energy, focus, flexibility or a rating. The more you add, the sharper these guides get.
optimize.nothingToOptimizeDescription: This period looks balanced — no heavy days, short nights or stranded tasks stood out.
optimize.allDismissed: All suggestions for this period are dismissed.
optimize.notUseful: Not useful — mute this kind of tip

overloadedDay.title: Heavy day: {day}
overloadedDay.body: Noticeably more scheduled time than a typical day — worth keeping some buffer.
overloadedDay.evidenceSummary: {duration} scheduled on {day} vs a {typical} typical day.
overloadedDay.evidenceThreshold: Fires when a day exceeds 1.5× the median active day of this and the previous period (at least 6h).
overloadedDay.evidenceThresholdSparse: Fires above 8h when there are too few active days for a typical-day baseline.

strandedFlexible.title: Movable items on {day}
strandedFlexible.body: {names} {count, plural, one {is} other {are}} marked movable — {lighterDay} looks lighter.
strandedFlexible.evidenceSummary: {count, plural, one {# movable item sits} other {# movable items sit}} on {day}, a day above your overload threshold; {lighterDay} has the least scheduled time.
strandedFlexible.evidenceThreshold: Fires when items marked movable or flexible fall on a day above the heavy-day threshold.

fragmentation.title: Days are getting choppier
fragmentation.body: More short scattered blocks than last period — batching similar items protects focus.
fragmentation.evidenceSummary: Average busy block is {duration}, down from {prev} last period.
fragmentation.evidenceSummaryShare: The share of short scattered blocks jumped vs the previous period.
fragmentation.evidenceThreshold: Fires when the short-block share jumps 15+ points, or the average block drops to ≤75% of last period's, over at least 5 blocks in each.

lateNight.title: Short night into {day}
lateNight.body: A late evening ran close to an early start — under 8 hours between them.
lateNight.evidenceSummary: {duration} of real elapsed time between the late end and the {day} early start.
lateNight.evidenceThreshold: Fires when under 8 hours separate an evening ending at 23:00 or later (or past midnight) from a 4–8 am start the next morning.

categoryDrift.title: {name} shifted {pts} pts
categoryDrift.body: {name}: share {direction, select, up {grew} other {shrank}} by {pts} points vs the previous period.
categoryDrift.evidenceSummary: {name} holds {pct}% of tracked time, vs {prevPct}% in the previous period.
categoryDrift.evidenceThreshold: Fires on a 15+ point share shift with at least 3h in either period and 8h tracked in both.

unscheduledTask.title: Due soon: {title}
unscheduledTask.body: High priority and due {day}, with no upcoming time blocked in this period.
unscheduledTask.evidenceSummary: “{title}” is high priority, due {day}, and has no upcoming block in this period's calendar.
unscheduledTask.evidenceThreshold: Fires for open high-priority tasks due within 7 days that have no upcoming scheduled block.

goalOverBudget.title: {name}: over budget
goalOverBudget.body: {name}: time has passed the cap you set for this period.
goalOverBudget.evidenceSummary: {duration} tracked against a {cap} cap (the weekly budget scaled to this period).
goalOverBudget.evidenceThreshold: Fires when tracked time passes an at-most goal's period-scaled cap.

goalUnderBudget.title: {name}: behind pace
goalUnderBudget.body: {name}: there's still room to reach the target — a block or two would close the gap.
goalUnderBudget.evidenceSummary: {actual} tracked where the elapsed share of the period implies {expected} to stay on pace for {target}.
goalUnderBudget.evidenceThreshold: Fires mid-window when tracked time is under the pace an at-least target implies.

forecastOverload.title: Next period already looks heavy
forecastOverload.body: More time is committed ahead than you typically track — worth rebalancing before it starts.
forecastOverload.evidenceSummary: {duration} already scheduled next period — {pct}% of your typical pace ({typical}/day over {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}).
forecastOverload.evidenceThreshold: Fires when committed time for the next window exceeds 110% of the typical-day pace.

anomaly.title: Out-of-pattern day: {day}
anomaly.body: {direction, select, high {Tracked time sat far above your usual day — worth a look at what piled up.} other {Tracked time sat far below your usual day — fine if it was a rest day, worth a look if not.}}
anomaly.evidenceSummary: {duration} on {day} — a robust z-score of {z} against the period's median day.
anomaly.evidenceThreshold: Fires at |z| ≥ 3 (median/MAD) over at least 14 active days, so single odd days on short ranges never alarm.

streakBroken.title: A {days, plural, one {#-day} other {#-day}} streak ended
streakBroken.body: You tracked time several days in a row earlier in this period — one small block today restarts the run.
streakBroken.metaLongest: longest run {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}
streakBroken.evidenceSummary: The longest run of consecutive active days this period reached {days}; the most recent day has nothing tracked.
streakBroken.evidenceThreshold: Fires when a streak of 5+ active days ends within the period.

sleepDebt.title: Sleep is running short
sleepDebt.body: Several recent nights came in under 7 hours — an earlier evening or two would pay it back.
sleepDebt.metaShort: {count, plural, one {# short night} other {# short nights}}
sleepDebt.evidenceSummary: {short} of your last {total} logged nights were under 7h (average {avg}).
sleepDebt.evidenceThreshold: Fires when 3 of the last 7 logged nights are under 7 hours.

restWindow.title: Room to breathe on {day}
restWindow.body: A heavy day, but your waking hours still leave a clear open stretch — enough to step away.
restWindow.evidenceSummary: {tracked} tracked on {day}; the largest open daytime stretch is {gap} from {time}.
restWindow.evidenceThreshold: Fires when a heavy day has an open waking-hours gap of an hour or more (the night window is excluded).

correlationInsight.title: {name} keeps rating low
correlationInsight.body: Time in {name} averaged {mean} of 4 satisfaction — worth a look at what makes it drag.
correlationInsight.evidenceSummary: {n, plural, one {# rated item} other {# rated items}} in {name} average {mean} of 4, duration-weighted. A pattern, not a cause — only you know why.
correlationInsight.evidenceThreshold: Fires when a context's duration-weighted satisfaction averages ≤2.0 of 4 over at least {min} rated items.

digest.label: Digest
digest.reading: Reading the period… usually about ten seconds.
digest.intro: A short written read of this period — what stood out and what to do about it.
digest.privacy: Sends summary statistics and context names only — never event details or sleep data.
digest.footer: Written for {label} from summary statistics only.

heatmap.caption24: Tracked time by weekday and hour of day
heatmap.captionBands: Tracked time by weekday in 4-hour bands

dayDetail.nothingScheduled: Nothing scheduled in the current insights filter.
dayDetail.summary: {ms} tracked across {count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}} (current filter).

goals.title: Goals
goals.empty: Set a weekly target (or budget) for a context to see progress here — the tick marks the goal, the thin line marks today's pace.
goals.srTarget: {name}: {actual} of the {target} target for this period — {judgment}.
goals.srBudget: {name}: {actual} of the {target} budget for this period — {judgment}.
goals.weeklyDescription: Hours per week per context — a target to reach, or a budget to stay under. Goals are shared: you and your partner plan against the same numbers.
goals.noGoals: No goals yet — add one below.
goals.pickContext: Context to add a goal for

sleep.privacyNote: Sleep is personal — this tab shows yours only, and your check-ins are never visible to your partner.
sleep.debtHeadline: You're carrying about {ms} of recent sleep debt.
sleep.debtSupport: Tonight's recommendation below already accounts for it.
sleep.loadError: We couldn't load your sleep logs. Check your connection and try again.
sleep.noDataDescription: Log a night below, or mark your nightly events as inactive in the event dialog — Insights derives your nights from them automatically.
sleep.correlationsEmpty: Once you've logged enough nights alongside rated days, this shows how your sleep duration and quality track with the next day's load, focus and satisfaction.
